title: Coatings containing carbon composite fillers and methods of manufacture
abstract: An article comprises a substrate, a coating disposed on a surface of the substrate. The coating comprises a carbon composite dispersed in one or more of the following: a polymer matrix; a metallic matrix; or a ceramic matrix. The carbon composite comprises carbon and a binder containing one or more of the following: SiO 2 ; Si; B; B 2 O 3 ; a filler metal; or an alloy of the filler metal.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. An article comprising a substrate (2) and a coating (1) disposed on a surface of the substrate (2), the article characterized in that the coating comprises a carbon composite (3) dispersed in one or more of the following: a polymer matrix (4); a metallic matrix (4); or a ceramic matrix (4), wherein the carbon composite (3) comprises carbon preferably graphite and a binder containing one or more of the following: Si0 2 ; Si; B; B 2 0 ; a filler metal; or an alloy of the filler metal, and the filler metal comprises one or more of the following: aluminum; copper; titanium; nickel; tungsten; chromium; iron; manganese; zirconium; hafnium; vanadium; niobium; molybdenum; tin; bismuth; antimony; lead; cadmium; or selenium. 2. The article of claim 1, wherein the carbon composite (3) comprises at least two carbon micro structures; and a binding phase disposed between the at least two carbon microstructures. 3. The article of claim 2, wherein the binding phase comprises a binder layer and an interface layer bonding one of the at least two carbon microstructures to the binder layer, wherein the interface layer comprises one or more of the following: a C-metal bond; a C-B bond; a C-Si bond; a C-O-Si bond; a C-O-metal bond; or a metal carbon solution. 4. The article of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the coating (1) comprises a metallic matrix (4) including one or more of the following: Ni; Cu; Ag; Au; Sn; Zn; Fe; In; W; Ti; Co; Al; Mg; Cr; or Mo; or an alloy thereof. 5. The article of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the coating (1) comprises a ceramic matrix (4) comprising one or more of the following: an oxide-based ceramic; nitride- based ceramic; carbide-based ceramic; boride-based ceramic; or silicide-based ceramic. 6. The article of any one of claims 1 to 5, further comprising a binding layer disposed between the coating (1) and the substrate (2). 7. The article of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the substrate (2) comprises one or more of the following: a metal; a polymer; or ceramics. 8. The article of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the article is a coated bearing, a coated pipeline, a coated valve, a coated piston, or a coated shaft. 9. A method of coating a substrate (15), the method characterized by: depositing a matrix material (14) and a carbon composite (10) on a surface of a substrate (15), 

 the carbon composite (10) comprising carbon and a binder; the binder comprising one or more of the following: Si0 2 ; Si; B; B 2 0 ; a metal; or an alloy of the metal; and the metal comprising one or more of the following: aluminum; copper; titanium; nickel; tungsten; chromium; iron; manganese; zirconium; hafnium; vanadium; niobium; molybdenum; tin; bismuth; antimony; lead; cadmium; or selenium. 10. The method of claim 9, further characterized by combining a matrix material (14) with a carbon composite (10) to form a coating composition (12), and depositing a matrix material (14) and a carbon composite (10) comprises disposing the coating composition (12) on a surface of the substrate(15). 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the matrix material (14) comprises a material or a precursor for the polymer matrix, and combing the matrix material (14) with the carbon composite (10) comprises dispersing the carbon composite (10) in a dispersion of the matrix material (14). 12. The method of claim 10, wherein the matrix material (14) comprises a material or a precursor for the metallic or ceramic matrix, and the matrix material (14) is combined with a carbon composite (10) and an organic compound to form a coating composition (12) in the form of a paste. 13. The method of claim 9, wherein disposing the matrix material (14) and the carbon composite (10) is an all-solid state coating process. 14. The method of claim 9, wherein disposing the matrix material (14) and the carbon composite (10) comprises thermal spraying the matrix material (14) and the carbon composite (10) simultaneously on the surface of the substrate (15). 15. The method of claim 9, wherein the coating (12) comprises a metallic matrix, and depositing the matrix material (14) and the carbon composite (10) is conducted via electroplating.
